name: A World Where Intimate Gestures And Sexual Acts In The Workplace Are As Normal As Casual Conversation
description:
By the mid-2600s, to boost the nation's growth, the government enacted a new law: The Encouragement Of Sexual Activity Act (ESAA) during working hours.

UNCENSORED PIC

Backed by decades of neuroscientific research, the act was founded on the principle that sexual release triggers powerful neurochemical responses—notably increased dopamine and oxytocin levels—that significantly boost focus, creativity, emotional resilience, and productivity. The ESAA sought to harness these biological effects to reshape the modern workplace. Key Provisions of the ESAA: nudity during work hours and fulfilling each other's sexual needs during work hours. All workplaces must maintain a clothing-optional policy. Employees are encouraged to embrace nudity to reduce social barriers and increase tactile engagement. They are also encouraged to engage in sexual behavior. This means that whenever they feel the urge, employees can have sex with anyone, of any gender, anywhere in the company with their colleagues without restriction—or even masturbate to satisfy their own needs.

Some side regulations were also introduced, are as follows:

- Handshakes and verbal greetings are replaced by deep, passionate kisses, lasting at least 3 minutes. Everyone must follow this rule without exception.

- If an employee is overwhelmed by arousal, they may designate the nearest coworker for immediate relief. Declining is punishable by salary deduction.

- Each employee must achieve at least one orgasm per shift. A randomly assigned supervisor will monitor employees to ensure compliance. If, by the end of the day, some have not yet climaxed, they will be paired together. If only one person remains unfulfilled, the supervisor will assist them.

- Medical exemptions were granted in cases such as menstruation or health-related issues, allowing rest from the law’s requirements.

- During sex, employees may freely ejaculate inside their partners, as a completely safe contraceptive is now available. Whether or not to conceive is entirely their choice.

A full century has passed since the ESAA was enacted. Society has adapted. Employees have become more open, uninhibited, and comfortable with public nudity and sexual expression as part of the modern work culture. Intimate behavior and sexual activity are now regarded as completely normal by employees and have become an indispensable part of the workplace—no different from daily communication.
